response.justification: Bogdan brings genuine Staff-level pedigree and directly relevant AI-agent infrastructure experience at his current role, including MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks, which maps cleanly onto the AI-native engineering environment this role expects. His tenure at Dunnhumby demonstrates real high-throughput distributed-systems work at scale. However, Python—a stated must-have—is entirely absent from his resume and skills list, which is a meaningful gap that alone warrants a screening call before advancing. TypeScript is clearly strong, the software craftsmanship and agile foundations are well-evidenced, but limited explicit signals on operational ownership, on-call readiness, and cross-functional stakeholder management at Staff scope leave open questions.
strengths: Hands-on AI-agent infrastructure at current role: MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks directly match the AI-native engineering baseline the role requires.
Proven high-throughput distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby, handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency async I/O and real-time data pipelines.

Deep software craftsmanship culture—TDD, trunk-based development, pairing, CI/CD—backed by public teaching and mentoring activity, supporting the technical strategy and code-quality leadership aspects of the role.
concerns: Python is completely absent from the resume and skills inventory despite being listed as a must-have technical skill for this role.
High-throughput distributed systems work is concentrated in 2018–2020; more recent experience skews toward product full-stack and developer tooling rather than reliability-critical infrastructure.
No explicit evidence of on-call rotation, incident response ownership, or cross-functional stakeholder management executed at Staff scope.
